question_title: Will Copernicus (C3S) report that March 2026 was the warmest March on record globally?
summary: March 2026 will almost certainly NOT be the warmest March on record. ERA5 daily data for all 31 days of March 2026 shows average global temperature of ~12.955°C, which is 1.1°C cooler than March 2024 (the current record at ~14.064°C) and ~1.0°C cooler than March 2025 (2nd warmest). Even the warmest single day in March 2026 was cooler than the coolest day of March 2024. This enormous gap is consistent with Jan/Feb 2026 both ranking only 5th warmest for their months, and the ENSO transition from La Niña to neutral providing cooling relative to 2024's El Niño. Regional heat waves (US, Moscow) are insufficient to change the global monthly average. The only scenario for YES would be a fundamental data processing error, which I assign very low probability.

factors.description: ERA5 daily data shows March 2026 averaging ~12.955°C vs March 2024's ~14.064°C — a 1.1°C gap. Even the warmest day in 2026 (13.184°C) is below the coolest day in 2024 (13.498°C). Mathematically impossible for March 2026 to be warmest.
January and February 2026 were both only 5th warmest for their respective months per C3S, confirming 2026 is far from record-breaking territory
ENSO transitioning from La Niña to neutral in early 2026 — provides cooling influence compared to the El Niño that boosted 2024's record-breaking months
Record US heat wave and Moscow warmth in March 2026 are striking but regional; insufficient to overcome the ~1°C global monthly average gap
Sea surface temperatures hit a daily record on March 22, 2026, showing some warming signal — but SST averages for the month remain below 2024 peaks

reasoning:
**Forecast: 2% probability (YES)**

The evidence is overwhelming that March 2026 will **not** be reported as the warmest March on record. The daily ERA5 temperature data — the same reanalysis underpinning C3S reports — shows March 2026 running approximately 1.1°C cooler than the record-holder (March 2024) across all 31 days. This gap is insurmountable. The 2% residual accounts only for the unlikely possibility of a fundamental data error.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Copernicus (C3S) report that March 2026 was the warmest March on record globally?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42813)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves Yes if, after the time the question opens and before May 1, 2026, a Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) publication states that March 2026 was the warmest March on record globally (or equivalent unambiguous wording such as “the warmest March in the record” for global temperature). The primary source is the C3S March 2026 monthly climate page (or its March 2026 bulletin page) at: https://climate.copernicus.eu/climate-bulletin
fine_print: If Copernicus text is ambiguous, resolve per the credible-sources [policy](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).
